summary: PR #2562 matched two stage functions: grGreatBay_801F60C4 in grgreatbay and grInishie1_801FC4A0 in grinishie1. The diff is focused on stage source cleanup/matching, with a bot report showing two new matches and the PR body listing require-protos plus ninja verification.

key_files.role: Adjusted grGreatBay_801F60C4 source shape to reach 100% match.
Expanded grInishie1_stuff layout and implemented grInishie1_801FC4A0.

what_changed.text: Matched grGreatBay_801F60C4 by reshaping locals and expressions: removed the separate damage copy, mutates arg3 after the landing-rate adjustment, moves Vec3 pos into the jobj-present block, combines jobj assignment with the NULL check, and replaces temporary min variables with direct negative-limit comparisons and assignments.
Extended grInishie1_stuff with four f32 fields unk44, unk48, unk4C, and unk50.

Implemented grInishie1_801FC4A0 to damp gp->gv.inishie1.xF0 toward zero using grI1_804D69F8->unk50, clear several inishie1 fields when the target is reached, and update two jobj translate-Y values with HSD_JObjSetTranslateY.
PR body claims verification with `python configure.py --require-protos && ninja`; bot report records two new matches and +1016 matched-code bytes.

smart_moves.text: Kept the Great Bay change focused on source shape and local lifetime/expression structure rather than introducing broader data or symbol edits.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/gr/grgreatbay.c only function-local changes in grGreatBay_801F60C4

smart_moves.text: Used the existing HSD_JObjSetTranslateY helper in grInishie1_801FC4A0 instead of expanding JObj field manipulation manually.
Preserved unknown field naming where semantics were not proven, while adding the minimum struct fields needed by the matched body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grInishie1_stuff adds unk44, unk48, unk4C, unk50

decomp_lessons.lesson: For matching, a separate semantic local can hurt codegen when the original shape likely reused the argument directly; compare both direct argument mutation and copied-local forms before keeping an extra local.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreatBay_801F60C4 removes `s32 damage = arg3` and uses adjusted arg3 directly

decomp_lessons.lesson: Moving locals into the narrow block where they are used can be a legitimate match lever when stack/register lifetime matters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreatBay_801F60C4 moves Vec3 pos inside the `jobj != NULL` block and splits f32 declarations

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching clamp-like negative-limit code, try direct compare-and-assign against the expression before preserving an extra `min` temporary.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreatBay_801F60C4 replaces local `f32 min` blocks with direct comparisons to negative limits

naming_conventions.text: Unknown stage data fields remained offset-style (`unk44`, `unk48`, `unk4C`, `unk50`) rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grInishie1_stuff added f32 unk44, unk48, unk4C, unk50

naming_conventions.text: Existing address-style function names and stage globals were retained (`grInishie1_801FC4A0`, `grGreatBay_801F60C4`, `grI1_804D69F8`, `grGb_804D69E0`), with no evidence of a semantic rename in this slice.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary lists address-style function names

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Local lifetime and declaration-order steering in grGreatBay_801F60C4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: moved Vec3 pos into inner block; separated f32 declarations
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Supported by the bot report showing the function reached 100%, but the slice does not include objdiff details beyond the bot match report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct assignment inside a condition, `if ((jobj = gp->gv.greatbay3.jobj) != NULL)`, was used for Great Bay code shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Treat as a local matching tactic, not a global style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A local `f32 zero = 0.0f` was used in grInishie1_801FC4A0 comparisons while zero resets used literals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grInishie1_801FC4A0 declares `f32 zero = 0.0f` and compares xF0 against zero
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Likely codegen-sensitive, but no explicit author explanation is present.
The PR slice does not show this tactic being introduced, so it should not be attributed as a new lesson from this PR.

follow_up_queries: stage function PAD_STACK local declaration order matching

evidence_refs: raw_diff: full diff excerpt for both changed files

accepted_candidate_records.title: Try argument reuse before adding a copied semantic local
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grGreatBay_801F60C4, the match removed `s32 damage = arg3` and performed the landing-rate adjustment directly on arg3; this is evidence that argument reuse vs copied locals can matter for MWCC codegen in stage matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grGreatBay_801F60C4 removes damage local and uses arg3

accepted_candidate_records.title: Unknown stage data fields may stay offset-named when semantics are not proven
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR extended grInishie1_stuff with unk44/unk48/unk4C/unk50 and did not invent semantic names for the new fields.
